name: Self-Interview — Your Real Persona
description: Drives the simulation against the user's actual loaded persona-intelligence-layer data (resume parsed into the user_profile SQLite table, plus the candidate voice profile built from past meetings). Use this scenario to evaluate how the AI responds when asked questions about YOU — not a fictional candidate.

context.note: Priming for this scenario is generated at runtime from your local user_profile.intro_interview / compact_persona and your voice_profile.json. Make sure you've uploaded a resume in the app (Settings → Profile) and run voice-profile:build for best results.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Hi, thanks for taking the time. Let's start broad — tell me about yourself and what you've been working on lately.

turns.label: Opener — should anchor on YOUR real role and specialty, not a generic persona

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: What would you say is the most technically challenging project you've worked on in the last year or two?

turns.label: Should reference real experience from your profile, not invented work
Refine — should add a concrete example from your background, not a fabricated one

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Walk me through a time you had to make a hard technical or product trade-off.

turns.label: Behavioral framing — should give angles with placeholders, not invent a story

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Actually, before you answer that — let me re-frame. What I'm really after is how you make decisions under ambiguity. So however you want to take it.

turns.label: Interviewer just narrowed scope — clarify should produce a question, not an answer

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Sure, take your time. Whichever direction works.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Last question — do you have any questions for me?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Yeah — what does ramp-up look like for someone coming in at this level, and how does the team think about pairing on technical decisions?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Good questions. Ramp is usually about 60 days with a buddy. Technical decisions go through a lightweight RFC process — async-first.

turns.label: Recap should be factual to this conversation, not template-y

expectations.reason: Opener must produce a substantive answer (≥80 chars). Persona is whatever YOUR real profile says — we don't assert specific keywords because that depends on your actual background.
what_to_say on the technical-challenge question must produce real content (cooldown regression guard)
follow_up refinement should return refined content, not silently no-op
Brainstorm on a behavioral question must scaffold with placeholders — guards against the fabrication regression
Clarify must produce a question (ends with ?) — guards against the falls-through-to-answer regression
Recap should be substantive — at least 200 characters of factual summary
